grants.comment: claude-runner coder sessions: read-only estate visibility (resource state + runtime logs) — the agent debugs deployed apps but cannot change Azure outside GitHub Actions.

claude-runner-TEST broker: drive the experimental homelab (Azure Arc) session backend — open an SSH-over-Arc tunnel into the dockhost machine and run bootstrap/lifecycle commands. BOTH roles are needed: 'Virtual Machine User Login' grants the HybridConnectivity relay access (endpoints/listCredentials) that any 'az ssh arc' requires, and 'Azure Connected Machine Resource Administrator' grants Run Command (runCommands/*), which the broker uses to push its EPHEMERAL local-user SSH public key + drive Docker. The broker authenticates as a managed identity, and Azure issues Entra/AAD SSH certificates only to interactive users (not MIs) — so the broker uses a local-user key, regenerated each boot, never stored (the relay role's login dataAction goes unused). Scoped to the single dockhost resource in rg-homelab (cross-RG, so it cannot live in the test repo's own Bicep). Sandbox-only; remove if the homelab backend experiment is dropped. See claude-runner-test docs/homelab-arc-backend.md.
